clinical_notes: Patient presents with exertional chest tightness and palpitations. HbA1c 9.8%, fasting glucose 160 mg/dL confirming poorly controlled Type 2 Diabetes. LDL 196 mg/dL, HDL 33 mg/dL, Triglycerides 340 mg/dL. ECG shows mild ST-segment changes. Peripheral neuropathy early stage in both feet. Father had MI at age 60. Referred to cardiology and endocrinology for dual management.

Patient presents with persistent severe headaches, blurred vision and bilateral pedal oedema. BP 185/115 mmHg on 3 separate readings. eGFR 28 mL/min/1.73m² consistent with CKD Stage 4. Creatinine 3.2 mg/dL, urea 58 mg/dL. Proteinuria 2.8 g/day. Renal ultrasound shows bilateral cortical thinning. Nephrology and hypertension clinic referral initiated.
